group.value: Group C — opponents Brazil, Morocco, Scotland

fifa_ranking.value: Ranked around 83rd in the world — the lowest-ranked side in Group C

qualification.value: Topped the Third Round of CONCACAF qualifying ahead of Honduras (11 pts from 6 — 3W 2D 1L) to seal direct qualification — and played ZERO home games during the campaign: ongoing conflict in Haiti forced every 'home' fixture to be staged in Curacao

wc_history.value: First World Cup appearance since 1974 — a 52-year absence ended

key_players.value: Duckens Nazon (striker, all-time top scorer; JOINT-top scorer in CONCACAF qualifying with 6, tied with Guatemala's Oscar Santis, incl. a hat-trick off the bench vs Costa Rica). Note: 6 was the CONCACAF-region high only — global qualifying top scorer was Haaland (16).

wc_result_md1.value: Lost their opening match 0-1 to Scotland on June 13, 2026 (John McGinn 28') at Gillette Stadium, Foxborough

image.attribution: Flag of Haiti — public domain via Wikimedia Commons
